title: Proclamation No. 444, s. 1957
text:
MALACAÑANG RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 444

DECLARING THE PERIOD FROM OCTOBER 6 TO 12, 1957, AS BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONAL WOMEN’S WEEK

In recognition of the important role being played by business and professional women in the promotion of the economic and social welfare of our country and people, I, Carlos P. Garcia, President of the Philippines, do hereby declare the period from October 6 to 12, 1957, as Business and Professional Women’s Week. I call upon all citizens and residents of the Philippines, irrespective of nationality or creed, to cooperate in the celebration of this week so that the contribution of business and professional women to the economy of the country as well as the problems brought about by their dual role as home-makers and earners will be brought to the attention of the whole country.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 17th day of September, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and fifty-seven, and of the Independence of the Philippines, the twelfth.

CARLOS P. GARCIA President of the Philippines

By the President:

FORTUNATO DE LEON Executive Secretary

Source: University of the Philippines, College of Law Library
